body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-11 (snapshot 2026-07-11_2)
Regime data: VIX 15.03 | HY OAS 270 | Breadth 90.9%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime shows a low VIX at 15.03 with a steep contango structure (9D at 11.15 vs 3M at 18.57), signaling subdued near-term volatility but elevated longer-term uncertainty, while HY spreads remain elevated at 270 bps, reflecting cautious credit conditions amid rising rates (US10Y at 4.54%). SPY is perched just below its all-time high at $754.95 with moderate IV at 19.05% and positive sentiment, suggesting a bullish but fragile tape primed for a breakout if momentum sustains. NVDA at $210.96 with IV north of 45% and strong positive sentiment is the clear AI euphoria proxy, supported by the ongoing IPO boom highlighted by SK Hynix’s strong debut, which could fuel further tech sector upside. AMZN at $245.34 shows neutral sentiment and no fresh catalysts, implying limited near-term directional conviction. The key catalyst to watch is the upcoming bank earnings next week (BAC, JPM, WFC, GS, MS), as any signs of credit stress or cautious guidance could quickly unravel risk appetite and pull SPY back from record highs. The main risk is a potential volatility spike if credit conditions worsen or if the AI IPO enthusiasm fades abruptly. System is in warmup mode with IV rank filters inactive, so expect less signal clarity until full activation.

SPY.sentiment_driver: S&P 500 inches away from record high
SPY.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

NVDA.sentiment_driver: AI euphoria persists with IPO boom
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Why the 2026 IPO boom is about to broaden beyond AI mega-deals
SK Hynix shares jump in marquee US debut as AI euphoria persists
S&P 500 Snapshot: Inches Away From Record High

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with major reports from BAC, JPM, WFC, and GS on July 14, followed by MS on July 15. Investors will focus on credit quality and margin trends amid mixed expectations, especially the cautious tone around WFC versus the slightly positive outlook for GS. The financial sector’s performance will set the tone for broader risk sentiment, influencing SPY’s near-term direction given banks’ market sensitivity. Watch for any signs of credit stress or upside surprises that could also impact tech and consumer discretionary risk appetite, including early signals for AMZN.
narratives.next_week: Next week features key Communication Services earnings with GOOGL and T on July 22, alongside TSLA in Consumer Discretionary. GOOGL’s AI investment focus and moderate growth expectations are critical for the tech sector’s momentum, directly relevant to NVDA’s valuation as AI leadership narratives evolve. TSLA’s growth outlook tempered by margin pressure could weigh on discretionary sentiment and SPY’s cyclicals exposure. Real estate and industrials also report, but the tech and consumer discretionary earnings will be the main cross-asset drivers.
narratives.next_month: The following month brings heavyweight tech earnings from MSFT, AAPL, AMZN, and META between July 29-30, alongside the FOMC meeting and July PCE data. Strong cloud and AI growth expected from MSFT and AMZN underpin bullish views on NVDA and the broader tech sector, while the FOMC’s likely hold and inflation data will calibrate risk appetite across equities and fixed income. Investors should monitor inflation surprises that could pressure multiples and weigh on SPY, with energy and materials earnings adding nuance to sector rotation themes ahead of August’s NFP report.
